project.name: opencode
project.description: AI-powered development tool — agent orchestration engine with multi-lane execution model

typecheck.note: TypeScript Go native compiler (typescript-native-preview), not tsc

entry_points.dev_server: src/index.ts (via bun run --conditions=browser, file not yet committed or generated)
entry_points.bin_wrapper: bin/opencode (200-line Node.js shim that delegates to Bun)

CLI entry points — effect-cmd, bootstrap, network, logo, upgrade, ui, error, heap

database_backends.schema_files: *.pg.sql.ts, *.duckdb.ts, schema.ts — mixed Drizzle schema defs + raw SQL

notable_architectural_observations: No src/index.ts entry point exists — dev script references it but file is not committed, suggesting it's a generated or future file
Two ACP implementations coexist: src/acp/ (legacy) and src/acp-next/ (vNext rewrite)
Tests live in test/ mirroring src/ structure, plus inline tests in src/campaign/
Storage layer has 3 backends (SQLite, PG, DuckDB) with conditional imports via package.json #imports
Effect is the central runtime abstraction — layers, contexts, services used pervasively (1260 matches in 129 files)
plugin system supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic via ai-sdk, plus first-party: xAI, DigitalOcean, Cloudflare, Azure, GitHub Copilot)
The tool subsystem alone has ~72 files — richest part of the codebase
Multi-lane campaign engine is a distinct orchestration domain with its own state machine, predicates, role contracts
